name: Character-Based LCD Display
description: `lcd_pcf8574` is for LCD displays with a PCF8574 GPIO expander module connected to all the data pins. This has the benefit that you only need to connect two data wires to the ESP instead of the six or ten as with the [lcd_gpio Component](https://esphome.io/components/display/lcd_display#lcd-gpio). The communication happens via [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c), you need to have an `i2c:` section in your configuration.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) address of the PCF8574 chip, defaults to `0x3F`.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to re-draw the screen. Defaults to `1s`.

config_entries.label: Dimensions
config_entries.description: The dimensions of the display with `COLUMNSxROWS`. If you're not sure, power the display up and just count them.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: The lambda to use for rendering the content on the display. See [Rendering Lambda](https://esphome.io/components/display/lcd_display#display-lcd_lambda) for more information.
